results.item: AUDIT ITEM 1 — "All config variables consolidated so any change is easy."
results.evidence: Infrastructure for consolidation exists but did NOT displace the scattered reads — the two coexist. settings.py (98 lines) defines ModelSettings (12 typed model fields only, lines 34-45), get_model_settings(), and resolve(key)=os.getenv(key, CONFIG_DEFAULTS[key]) (settings.py:88-98). config_defaults.py holds a CONFIG_DEFAULTS registry of 871 single-line-default keys. resolve() is genuinely broadly adopted: imported in ~130 modules, ~832 resolve("KEY") call-sites. BUT: 877 raw os.getenv/os.environ reads still live OUTSIDE settings.py/config_defaults.py across ~197 files. Same files use both paths — e.g. state.py imports resolve (line 14), has ~92 resolve() calls AND ~23 raw os.getenv reads. DOUBLE SOURCE OF TRUTH: 125 distinct keys are defined in BOTH the registry AND a hardcoded raw literal; proof — PG_SECTION_WRITER_MAX_TOKENS='16384' at config_defaults.py:682 AND os.getenv(...,"16384") at state.py:103, so editing the registry does NOT change state.py:103. CONFLICTING DEFAULT: PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY read raw as "10" at graph_v2.py:63 but "5" at state.py:94 and retrieval/fetch_limiter.py:27, absent from the registry (3 hardcoded literals to change). SECRETS read raw, not centralized: OPENROUTER_API_KEY, EXA_API_KEY, SERPER_API_KEY, ZYTE_API_KEY, SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY, POLARIS_GPG_KEY_ID, PG_CREDIBILITY_JUDGE_API_KEY, etc. Config is split across .env + config_defaults.py registry + ~877 hardcoded literals — NOT a single change-point.
results.gaps: There is NOT a single place to change any setting. (1) 877 raw os.getenv reads remain outside the central modules; changing the registry does not affect them. (2) 125 keys are double-defined (registry literal + hardcoded call-site literal) so a registry edit silently no-ops at the raw site — a correctness hazard, not just cosmetic. (3) Conflicting defaults (PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY 10 vs 5 vs 5) live only as scattered hardcoded literals with no central owner. (4) API-key secrets are read raw with no central/typed accessor. (5) get_model_settings() typed layer covers only 12 model keys out of ~282 distinct env keys in use.
results.codex_verdict: CONFIG-NOT — "877 raw reads remain outside central modules; the registry has 871 keys; settings.py resolves via os.getenv but defines only 12 model fields (lines 34-45); 16384 is duplicated at state.py:103/config_defaults.py:682; fetch defaults conflict at graph_v2.py:63 versus state.py:94 and retrieval/fetch_limiter.py:27, with zero registry entries. The claimed 127 double-defined keys was not reproduced: exact intersection was 125."
results.recommendation: SCOPE ONLY (do not implement). To close the gap to true consolidation: (1) Mechanically migrate the ~877 raw os.getenv call-sites to resolve()/get_*_settings(), removing the hardcoded literal at each site so the registry becomes the sole default source — highest-value step, eliminates the 125 double-definitions. (2) Resolve the conflicting defaults (e.g. PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY) by choosing one authoritative value, registering it, and pointing all 3 sites at resolve(). (3) Add a typed Secrets accessor (like ModelSettings) for the *_KEY/_TOKEN group and route the raw secret reads through it. (4) Add a CI guard (grep/AST test) that fails on any new raw os.getenv outside settings.py to prevent regression. (5) Expand the typed settings layer beyond the 12 model fields so common domains (retrieval, synthesis, token budgets) get validated accessors rather than string resolve(). Note the migration must preserve the documented byte-identical contract (fresh env read per call, not a cached module-level instance).

results.item: AUDIT ITEM 2 — "Checkpoint system across ALL connections/stages"
results.evidence:
TWO separate checkpoint systems exist. (A) File-based snapshots in the PRODUCTION per-query pipeline scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py::run_one_query (~L5141), driven by --resume (argparse L21731, RESUME-FROM-NEAREST). (B) A LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver checkpointer in src/polaris_graph/checkpoint_manager.py that is UNWIRED from the sweep — it serves only the separate graph.py/live_server.py UI pipeline (Codex: graph.py:1427, live_server.py:2358). The compose script scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py contains ZERO checkpoint/snapshot/resume machinery (my grep returned empty; Codex confirms it directly overwrites report.md).

STAGE-BY-STAGE MAP (production sweep --resume path):
- RETRIEVAL/FETCH: fetch_snapshot.json — CHECKPOINT YES, RESUME RE-ENTERS YES. Saved at L12111 (_save_fetch_snapshot), loaded L9545 when no later corpus snapshot; skips STORM/fetch/merge, re-enters at selection.
- CORPUS-APPROVAL/SELECTION: corpus_snapshot.json — CHECKPOINT YES, RESUME RE-ENTERS YES. Preferred over fetch (L9541); saved L15830 (_save_corpus_snapshot); restores evidence_for_gen (L13373), re-enters at generation.
- OUTLINE: PARTIAL. Only storm_outline.json is production-checkpointed (write L10512 write_storm_outline_checkpoint, restore L9559 load_storm_outline_checkpoint, seeded into _storm_outline L10424). The dedicated cp4_outline_snapshot module (src/polaris_graph/generator/outline_checkpoint.py) has build/write/LOAD funcs, but load_cp4_outline_snapshot (L131) is called ONLY by scripts/orchestrator_lab/outline_lab.py + tests — never by run_honest_sweep_r3.py. OutlineAgent (outline/outline_agent.py) only calls write_cp4 (L612) and is NOT invoked by the sweep; Codex adds the production generator call doesn't pass checkpoint_dir, so even that write is inert in production. So the true composition-outline stage is neither checkpointed nor resumed.
- SECTION-GENERATION: postgen_checkpoint.json — WRITTEN (L16172) + LOADED (L9551 load_a12_checkpoint) but DATA-ONLY OBSERVABILITY, NOT a re-entry point. The generation_snapshot reuse path (ITEM 5, PG_RESUME_REUSE_POSTGEN, L15893/L8984) exists but production never calls save_generation_snapshot and Gate-B leaves the flag OFF (Codex: run_gate_b.py:1417) — so generation is NOT operationally resumable; a corpus-snapshot resume RE-RUNS generation from scratch.
- VERIFICATION: postverify_checkpoint.json — WRITTEN (L18448) + LOADED (L9551) but SURFACED ONLY, does NOT skip into the 4-role/D8 judge. Verification+judge RE-RUN from scratch on resume (by design, §-1.3 no-verdict-replay invariant).
- RENDER: NO checkpoint. report.md is idempotently overwritten; self-correcting on --resume (L7452, finalizer-backstop marker detection). No distinct re-entry.
results.gaps: Not a complete "all-stages" checkpoint system. Effective production re-entry lands at exactly TWO seams: post-fetch and post-selection. Everything downstream (generation, verification, judge, render) always RE-RUNS from the reloaded corpus. Specific gaps: (1) OUTLINE stage has a purpose-built durable checkpoint (cp4_outline_snapshot with a working loader) that is NOT wired into the production sweep — write-only in outline_agent, and outline_agent isn't even called by the sweep; the generator call omits checkpoint_dir so the write is inert. Only the coarser storm_outline scaffold survives. (2) SECTION-GENERATION checkpoint (postgen) and the generation_snapshot reuse path are written but never used as a re-entry point in the default/Gate-B slate (save_generation_snapshot never called; flag OFF) — a paid ~$2.79 generation is redone on every resume even though the drafts are on disk. (3) VERIFICATION checkpoint (postverify) is loaded but never short-circuits the judge. (4) The sqlite checkpoint_manager and the file-based system are two disjoint mechanisms; the richer sqlite rewind/list/get facility does not apply to the benchmark pipeline. (5) --resume argparse help (L21731) is STALE — documents only fetch+corpus snapshots, omitting the storm/postgen/postverify artifacts. Note: gaps 2 and 3 are INTENTIONAL under the §-1.3 no-verdict-replay invariant (a resume must re-run every faithfulness gate), so they are design choices, not bugs — but they mean "checkpoint across ALL stages" is not literally achieved.
results.codex_verdict: CHECKPOINT-PARTIAL — "fetch and selected-corpus resume are operational; STORM outline restoration is partial; generation and verification artifacts are not production re-entry points; render has no checkpoint." Codex independently reproduced the full stage map and confirmed every point: sqlite manager unwired from the sweep (serves graph.py/live_server.py only), compose script clean of checkpoint machinery, cp4 outline loader called only by orchestrator_lab/tests, OutlineAgent write inert in production (generator omits checkpoint_dir), postgen/postverify surfaced-not-re-entered, generation_snapshot reuse OFF under Gate-B, render self-correcting not checkpointed, and --resume help text stale.
results.recommendation: SCOPE ONLY (do not implement): To reach a genuine "checkpoint across all stages" claim, three wiring gaps would need closing. (1) OUTLINE: pass checkpoint_dir into the production generator/OutlineAgent call and add a load_cp4_outline_snapshot re-entry in run_one_query's if resume: block (loader already exists and is verdict-guarded — this is a wiring, not a design task). (2) SECTION-GENERATION reuse: land the generator-side save_generation_snapshot hook (the consumer at L8984 already exists and fail-louds when the hook is absent) so PG_RESUME_REUSE_POSTGEN can actually skip re-generation; decide whether Gate-B should enable it. (3) VERIFICATION: if judge-only resume is desired, add a re-entry that reloads postverify data and re-runs ONLY the 4-role/D8 seam — but this must be weighed against the §-1.3 no-verdict-replay invariant (the current re-run-everything behavior is the conservative, faithfulness-safe choice). Lowest-effort correctness fix regardless of the above: update the stale --resume argparse help (L21731) to document all five artifacts and state which are re-entry points vs observability-only. Recommend consulting the user before any wiring change, since gaps 2-3 trade cost-savings against the frozen faithfulness invariant.

results.item: AUDIT ITEM 4 — raw-A: checker (evaluator gate) AND entailment (NLI faithfulness) "COMPLETELY off"
results.status: GAP-FOUND — audit claim is HALF WRONG. CHECKER (external evaluator gate): correctly OFF/not-run on raw-A, but this is by design (the evaluator is a separate offline scorer, never part of composition — so "off" is not a defect, it just never was a compose-path gate). ENTAILMENT (NLI faithfulness): the claim is FALSE — entailment/NLI is ON (enforce) on raw-A by default. So the two are NOT both "completely off"; entailment definitely runs.
results.evidence:
CHECKER/EVALUATOR GATE = NOT RUN on raw-A: scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py has 0 imports of external_evaluator (grep count 0); its only post-compose gate is _audit_citations (lines 102-121, 466-472), a pure citation tripwire, not the evaluator. In multi_section_generator.py the ONLY external_evaluator import is at line 8067 (`from ...external_evaluator import _next_real_sentence_end`) — a sentence-boundary helper for the PT11 numeric-cite text transform, NOT evaluate()/score()/verdict. Grep for evaluate(/run_evaluation/.score(/gate_report/EvaluatorResult in the generator returns nothing. Comment at multi_section_generator.py:5297 states outright: "the whole evaluator gate is demoted to advisory in four-role-seam mode".
ENTAILMENT/NLI = ON (enforce) on raw-A: gate flag is PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT with _DEFAULT_MODE = "enforce" at src/polaris_graph/clinical_generator/strict_verify.py:356 (flipped off->enforce per I-bug-095). The compose script NEVER sets it (grep confirms), and no .env/config override exists. verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2561-2622) reads _entailment_mode(); when mode in ("warn","enforce") it calls _get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span) — a REAL synchronous httpx OpenRouter NLI call (src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py, "synchronous httpx wrapper around an OpenRouter call"). Under enforce (line 2617) a NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED verdict appends "entailment_failed:" (2619-2622) which DROPS the sentence. strict_verify is the production gate for every section via verify_fn=verify_sentence_provenance (multi_section_generator.py:4849) and the evidence-base breadth path calls strict_verify directly (multi_section_generator.py:8218). Entailment judge is the 6th check, runs last after mechanical checks (comment at 2549-2560).
results.codex_verdict: PENDING — Codex (codex-sol) launched via `codex exec ... < /tmp/codex_gates.md` (bg task bdn07hq4y, monitor b467pbsge); had not emitted its GATES-{FULLY-OFF|PARTIAL|ON} token by the time the structured answer was required. Claude's expected Codex verdict on this evidence: GATES-PARTIAL (checker not-run-by-design / entailment ON) — which refutes the "both completely off" claim. Prompt file written and run; token not yet returned.
results.gaps: Audit claim conflates two very different things. (1) The "checker/evaluator gate is off" part is technically true but MISLEADING: the external evaluator was never a composition-time gate — it is an offline report scorer, so its absence on raw-A is expected, not a regression. Calling it "off" implies a disabled safety gate that never existed on this path. (2) The "entailment/NLI is completely off" part is simply WRONG: PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT defaults to "enforce", the compose script does not disable it, and the NLI judge is invoked per kept sentence and drops NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED sentences under enforce. Only remaining unverified nuance: the judge fails OPEN on judge_error (returns ("ENTAILED","judge_error:..."), provenance_generator.py:2591-2596) unless PG_ENTAILMENT_JUDGE_ERROR_ADVISORY is set — so a transport failure can silently pass a sentence, but that is fail-open-on-error, not "off".
results.recommendation: SCOPE ONLY (do not implement): (a) Correct the audit wording — entailment/NLI is NOT off on raw-A; it runs in enforce mode by default. Rephrase item 4 to "checker/external-evaluator gate does not run at compose time (by design — offline scorer); entailment/NLI runs (enforce)." (b) If the intent was to assert the evaluator SHOULD gate composition, that is a design proposal, not a defect finding, and needs separate justification. (c) The one genuine faithfulness-adjacent gap worth a follow-up probe: the entailment judge's fail-open-on-judge_error behavior (PG_ENTAILMENT_JUDGE_ERROR_ADVISORY default off) — quantify how many sentences on a real raw-A run pass via judge_error to confirm it is negligible. Await the Codex GATES-* token (bg task bdn07hq4y) to corroborate before finalizing the audit correction.

results.item: Deliverable Item 5 — single clean reproducible run command: scripts/run_raw_a.sh (tree /home/polaris/wt/phase-file)
results.evidence: Script: /home/polaris/wt/phase-file/scripts/run_raw_a.sh (created; +x). bash -n = SYNTAX_OK; --help = HELP_OK; missing-value guard fires ("option --corpus requires a value"). All 8 required knobs verified against the file: (1) LD_LIBRARY_PATH read via cat from the pinned LDPATH.txt and now exported as EXACTLY the pinned value (line ~93, inherited-append removed) — matches the file's real content; (2) PG_LOOPBACK_MODE=0; (3) PG_OUTLINE_AGENT=1, PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_SCORE_CHUNK=16, PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True; (4) PG_OUTLINE_MAX_TOKENS=131072, PG_OUTLINE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS=32768; (5) OPENROUTER_API_KEY + SERPER_API_KEY via python dotenv_values() only, never bash-sourcing .env — verified /workspace/POLARIS/.env line 304 IS `PG_EXA_CATEGORY=research paper` (unquoted space, the documented breakage); (6) unset PYTHONPATH; (7) interpreter /home/polaris/pipeline-env/bin/python; (8) --corpus/--rq-drb-task(=72)/--out-dir flags match driver argparse (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py lines 172-175) with defaults identical to the proven sibling _run_16way_s3gear329.sh (data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json). Gate did NOT run a paid compose (script exec's driver only on real invocation).
results.gaps: Corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json and DRB query.jsonl (third_party/deep_research_bench/data/prompt_data/query.jsonl) are git-ignored data artifacts not present in a fresh worktree, so the zero-arg default invocation needs the operator to supply a local corpus — this is an environment/data fact (defaults match the proven run verbatim), not a script defect; header now documents it. Script is untracked (git status ??) — expected, since the task scope is CREATE only, not commit.
results.codex_verdict: RUNSCRIPT-REVISE (initial pass on pre-fix script). Codex confirmed knobs 2-7 correct, dotenv used (never sourced), bash -n passes, correct interpreter+flags, no paid run. Its blocking findings: LD_LIBRARY_PATH appended inherited value (real reproducibility bug — FIXED); missing $2 guard (FIXED); knob-8 header lacked per-default rationale (FIXED); corpus/query.jsonl missing + script untracked (environment/scope facts, not changed).
results.recommendation: To reach a re-gate RUNSCRIPT-SOUND, the three script-level fixes are already applied. Remaining to make the default zero-arg run executable end-to-end (out of CREATE scope, do NOT implement here): stage the s3gear-329 corpus + DRB query.jsonl into the worktree (or set --corpus / a data path), and `git add scripts/run_raw_a.sh` if a committed reproducible entrypoint is desired. A quick optional re-run of the codex gate on the revised script would confirm SOUND.
